key_insight: LLM persona has two separable parts: stable aggregate trait scores and a frame-dependent geometric correlation structure that can collapse or recover depending on question ordering.
engineering_relevance: For reliable persona evaluation in LLM systems, prompts and questionnaires should be frame-aware, because aggregate scores can miss meaningful structure hidden in response geometry.
method: Build within-instance correlation matrices from IPIP-50 responses.
Analyze the matrices on SPD manifolds under manipulated question orderings.
Compare persona behavior across American and Chinese-American settings, with aligned vs randomized frames.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a 21% drop in aggregate Big Five scores under randomization, a 42% collapse in geometric features under frame misalignment, and recovery to 84% under shared frames.
watch_item: These results depend on questionnaire framing and simulated persona settings, so the geometric signal may not generalize without careful control of prompt order and evaluation design.
paper_title: The Dual Nature of LLM Persona: Aggregated Tendencies and Frame-Dependent Geometry
